key_insight: A time-aware tabular deep learning model can accurately predict inter-stop trolleybus energy use while also ranking the causal drivers of consumption and savings.
engineering_relevance: The framework can support route-level energy management, vehicle operation guidance, and infrastructure planning for dual-source trolleybus fleets.
method: Periodic time encoding + parameter-efficient batch-ensemble backbone for static and sequential tabular features
Bayesian optimization with tree-structured density estimation for hyperparameter tuning
Three-layer causal pipeline: feature attribution, LiNGAM causal direction discovery, and meta-learner treatment effect estimation
evidence_signal: On the Zurich trolleybus dataset with weather data, the model achieved MAPE 6.52% and R 0.982, outperforming ten statistical, tree-ensemble, and deep learning baselines.
watch_item: Causal findings depend on observational data and model assumptions, so they should be validated before being used as operational or policy thresholds.
paper_title: Inter-Stop Energy Prediction and Causal Driver Quantification for Dual-Source Trolleybuses via a Time-Aware Tabular Deep Learning Architecture
